meta.title: Uganda natural resources — map by business value
meta.ranking_method: Ordered by documented economic scale: (1) export/trade receipts where officially reported, (2) national production value and GDP share, (3) DGSM strategic deposits and industrial linkage. Coordinates are district/occurrence centroids for mapping — not concession boundaries.
meta.disclaimer: Gold export values include significant re-export/refining activity per Bank of Uganda and World Bank. Rankings show fiscal/commercial weight, not environmental priority or sustainable development score.

value_tiers.label: Tier 1 — Dominant trade & fiscal value
value_tiers.description: Largest export receipts or hydrocarbon sector scale.

value_tiers.label: Tier 2 — Strategic industry & major reserves
value_tiers.description: Large deposits, refineries, or cement/fertilizer backbone.

value_tiers.label: Tier 3 — Significant production / ASM value
value_tiers.description: Active mining with substantial employment or production value.

value_tiers.label: Tier 4 — High potential, lower current monetization
value_tiers.description: Documented resources (e.g. glass sand) with unrealized industrial projects.

resources.name: Gold
resources.economic_indicator: FY2023/24 gold export receipts ≈ US$3.09B (gross); net exports ≈ US$478M after raw gold imports (Ministry of Finance).

resources.name: Petroleum (Albertine Graben)
resources.economic_indicator: Lake Albert development (Tilenga/Kingfisher); dominant energy sector FDI — export via pipeline infrastructure.

resources.name: Copper & cobalt (Kilembe)
resources.economic_indicator: Historic flagship mine; government-led revival bids (Kasese). Strategic for electrification supply chains.

resources.name: Tin
resources.economic_indicator: Refineries (e.g. Mbarara); government cites ~3.5 Mt tin ore resources (verify via MEMD/DGSM before citing reserves).

resources.name: Iron ore
resources.economic_indicator: DGSM reports >150 Mt at Muko (60–65% Fe) and large Sukulu/Bukusu complexes — steel value chain potential.

resources.name: Phosphate (Sukulu)
resources.economic_indicator: Fertilizer feedstock; DGSM flags Sukulu as strategically important phosphate-niobium complex.

resources.name: Limestone & cement
resources.economic_indicator: Major industrial mineral by production volume (Hima, Tororo); drives construction sector inputs.

resources.name: Vermiculite
resources.economic_indicator: DGSM/UCEM: Namekhara reserves cited ≈54.9 Mt; active industrial mineral exports.

resources.name: Tungsten (wolfram)
resources.economic_indicator: Listed in UCEM priority minerals; production value growth in FY2021/22 national statistics (UCEM).

resources.name: Salt (Lake Katwe)
resources.economic_indicator: Artisanal salt production; local employment (DELVE); lower unit export value than metals.

resources.name: Construction sand & aggregates
resources.economic_indicator: ASM sector: UCEM cites ~US$850M ASM production value (sector-wide, not sand alone).

resources.name: High-purity silica (glass) sand
resources.economic_indicator: High strategic value for import substitution; limited realized revenue vs gold (Diimu project stalled).

resources.name: Niobium / tantalite (coltan)
resources.economic_indicator: DGSM: Sukulu niobium; columbite in Ntungamo/Bushenyi — technology metals.

resources.name: Dimension stone & marble
resources.economic_indicator: Widespread ASM quarries (DELVE); regional construction demand.
